params.description: Image A: Egg carton with 2 rows and 6 columns of eggs.
Image B: Parking lot with cars arranged in 3 rows and 4 columns.
Image C: Scattered marbles on a table, no alignment.
Image D: Window pane with 2 rows and 3 columns of glass panes.

beats.text: Arrays are not just in math. Look at these four pictures. Which ones are arrays?
Which pictures show arrays? Select all that apply.

validator.description: Student selected A, B, D and did not select C

beats.text: Egg carton, parking lot, window pane. Arrays are everywhere. Same rows and columns structure you have been working with.

elements.description: Image A: Egg carton with 2 rows and 6 columns of eggs.
Image B: Parking lot with cars arranged in 3 rows and 4 columns.
Image C: Scattered marbles on a table, no alignment.
Image D: Window pane with 2 rows and 3 columns of glass panes.

beats.text: Once you see arrays, you notice them everywhere. Same math, rows times columns, whether it is eggs, cars, or windows.
